LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING ON CROSS DEFENDANT OLIVER J. MAR, TRUSTEE OF THE MAR LIVING TRUST'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT, WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION. DEFENDANT DOES NOT ALLEGE THE CONTRACT ACCORDING TO ITS LEGAL EFFECT. DEFENDANT'S BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION IS CONCLUSORY AND DOES NOT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGE HOW PLAINTIFF IS OBLIGATED TO CURE THE MARCH 29, 2005 NOTICE OF VIOLATION. THE DEMURRER TO THE DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. THE DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS TO STATE A CLAIM IN THAT IT INCORPORATES BY REFERENCE PARAGRAPHS 1 THROUGH 7, AND DOESN'T SUFFICIENTLY DESCRIBE THE DUTIES WITH RESPECT TO THE EASEMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT OLIVER J. MAR, TRUSTEE OF THE MAR LIVING TRUST'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER TO THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. THE PARTIES' SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT DOES NOT EXPRESSLY ALLEGE THAT DEFENDANT IS RESPONSIBLE FOR CURING THE 3/29/05 NOTICE OF VIOLATION AND DEFENDANT'S INTERPRETATION OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IS NOT FACIALLY UNREASONABLE. SEE ARAGON-HAAS V. FAMILY SECURITY INS. SERVICES, INC., (1991) 231 CAL.APP.3D 232, 239 ("WHILE PLAINTIFF'S INTERPRETATION OF THE CONTRACT ULTIMATELY MAY PROVE INVALID, IT WAS IMPROPER TO RESOLVE THE ISSUE AGAINST HER SOLELY ON HER OWN PLEADING. 'IN RULING ON A DEMURRER, THE LIKELIHOOD THAT THE PLEADER WILL BE ABLE TO PROVE HIS ALLEGATIONS IS NOT THE QUESTION.'")(CITATION OMITTED). THE DEMURRER TO THE DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANT PLEADS AN ACTUAL CONTROVERSY. IN ADDITION, A GENERAL DEMURRER IS USUALLY NOT AN APPROPRIATE METHOD FOR TESTING THE MERITS OF A DECLARATORY RELIEF ACTION BECAUSE THE CROSS-COMPLAINANT IS ENTITLED TO A DECLARATION OF RIGHTS EVEN IF IT IS ADVERSE TO THE CROSS COMPLAINANT'S INTEREST. QUALIFIED PATIENTS ASS'N V. CITY OF ANAHEIM (2010) 187 CAL. APP. 4TH 734, 751. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

Off Calendar Sep-26-2013 Set For Trial On Apr-14-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Oct-10-2013 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Timothy M. Flaherty, Esq. of Morris Polich & Purdy LLP, Tel. 415.984.8507, for plaintiff and moving party;
John K. Kao, Esq. of John Kao Law Office, Tel. 415.362.9390, for defendant and opposing party;

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on CROSS DEFENDANT OLIVER J. MAR, TRUSTEE OF THE MAR LIVING TRUST'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the breach of contract cause of action is overruled. The parties' settlement agreement does not expressly allege that defendant is responsible for curing the 3/29/05 notice of violation and defendant's interpretation of the settlement agreement is not facially unreasonable. See Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc., (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239 ("While plaintiff's interpretation of the contract ultimately may prove invalid, it was improper to resolve the issue against her solely on her own pleading. 'In ruling on a demurrer, the likelihood that the pleader will be able to prove his allegations is not the question.'")(citation omitted). The demurrer to the declaratory relief cause of action is overruled. Defendant pleads an actual controversy. In addition, a general demurrer is usually not an appropriate method for testing the merits of a declaratory relief action because the cross-complainant is entitled to a declaration of rights even if it is adverse to the cross complainant's interest. Qualified Patients Ass'n v. City of Anaheim (2010) 187 Cal. App. 4th 734, 751. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-12-2013 Continued To Oct-09-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-14-2013 Continued To Sep-19-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT OLIVER J. MAR, TRUSTEE OF THE MAR LIVING TRUST'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is continued to September 19, 2013, to give the opposing party a chance to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. The opposing party shall immediately provide a courtesy copy of the opposition to Department 302, with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-02-2013 Continued To Aug-28-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-06-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael E. Hanna, Esq. of Morris Polich & Purdy, Tel. 415.984.8521 for plaintiff/cross defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The court adopted its tentative ruling on CROSS DEFENDANT OLIVER J. MAR, TRUSTEE OF THE MAR LIVING TRUST'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT, which was not contested. The Court grants Plaintiff's request for judicial notice. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the breach of contract cause of action. Defendant does not allege the contract according to its legal effect. Defendant's breach of contract cause of action is conclusory and does not sufficiently allege how plaintiff is obligated to cure the March 29, 2005 notice of violation. The demurrer to the declaratory relief cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. The declaratory relief cause of action fails to state a claim in that it incorporates by reference paragraphs 1 through 7, and doesn't sufficiently describe the duties with respect to the easement. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-14-2013 Continued To Jul-17-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-10-2013 Continued To May-29-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: timothy flaherty, esq., 415.984.8500, for plaintiff oliver mar, trustee of the mar living trust; No other appearances.

rULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS SHINE PROPERTIES, LLC, WWJW FAMILY, LLC, TON KIANG RESTAURANT, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied. The Court takes judicial notice of the settlement agreement. The settlement agreement is reasonably susceptible to plaintiff's interpretation and the allegations of the amended complaint do not conflict with the agreement. See Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc., (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239 ("While plaintiffs' interpretation of the contract may prove invalid, it [is] improper to resolve the issue against her solely on her own pleading. 'In ruling on a demurrer, the likelihood that the pleading will be able to prove his allegations is not the question.'"); see also Martinez v. Socoma Co., Inc., (1974) 11 Cal.3d 394, 400. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
